sections.content: World model: Clicking p in top panel did nothing. State unchanged. Tried clicking S, M, p in top panel - only M created a Y. Need to try interacting with the blob or the Y square at (3-6, 30-33). Let me try clicking the blob.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Find the blob
seg = current_frame.segmentation
blob = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b']
y_squares = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y']

if blob:
    bc = blob[0]['boundary']
    min_r = min(p[0] for p in bc)
    max_r = max(p[0] for p in bc)
    min_c = min(p[1] for p in bc)
    max_c = max(p[1] for p in bc)
    center_r = (min_r + max_r) // 2
    center_c = (min_c + max_c) // 2
    print(f"Blob: rows {min_r}-{max_r}, cols {min_c}-{max_c}, center ({center_r}, {center_c})")
    
    # Try clicking the center of the blob
    print(f"\nClicking blob center at ({center_r}, {center_c})")
    result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': center_r, 'col': center_c}])
    
    seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
    m2 = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'M']
    p2 = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'p']
    y2 = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y']
    s2 = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'S']
    blob2 = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b']
    print(f"\nAfter: M={len(m2)}, p={len(p2)}, Y={len(y2)}, S={len(s2)}")
    if blob2:
        print(f"Blob: {blob2[0]['pixels']}")
    print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
    print(f"Board changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
